Argentina anserina, also known as Patagonian thyme, is a type of flowering plant native to the Andes mountains. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Argentina anserina include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Argentina anserina include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and to support the immune system.
